titleInfo.title: Yingshan Xian Taipeng Shan shiku chubu yanjiu
營山縣太蓬山石窟初步研究
A Preliminary Study of the Mount Taipeng Cave Shrines in Yingshan County

mods.abstract:
Taipeng Shan 太蓬山, located 47 km north-east of Yingshan County 營山縣, at 731 m above sea level. Most inscriptions are distributed across the eastern and western face of Touming Cliff (Touming yan 透明岩/巖). The authors give an overview on the research history of the most famous inscription of the site, the colophon by An Lushan 安祿山, dated to 713 (also the earliest date found at the site). In July 2008, the sites was surveyed, and the niches were numbered (again). Touming Cliff, Donghan Gate 東寒門 and Xihan Gate 西寒門 together count 104 niches and more than 1470 sculpted images. Classification of types of caves/niches follows, and an overview on iconographical motifs is given. Niches K32 and K38 present Amitābha teaching to 52 bodhisattvas (weathered, bodhisattvas not countable).
Buddhist inscriptions, five in total. K7: Diamond Sutra, illegible. p. 60: K7. Diamond Sutra and K8. Diamond Sutra, illegible and transcription and identification of the two phrases about the vajra beings: 第二奉请辟毒金刚，第三奉请黄随求金刚
K9 (commonly named Dagu Cave 打鼓洞): fragments of a sutra text, illegible. K13: Diamond Sutra, ca. 5.000 characters K53: Fragments of 妙法蓮華經普賢菩薩勸發品第二十八 (T.262 or T.264?) 方便陀羅尼…滿三…即于佛前而說咒曰…阿婆…帝二 (T.262, 9: 61b5-27)
6 niches have votive inscriptions 造像題記: K13 (outside, on the left-hand side of the niche) 普濟寺碑記dated 907 (transcribed p. 60). Connection to the belief in the Ten Kings of Hell. K16 (located on the right-hand side of K13; outside the niche K16, to the left-and right-hand side on the cliff) right-hand side: 馮可振造像碑記dated 895 (well preserved, transcribed p. 61) Feng Kezhen donated the thousand-eyed and thousand-arms Guanshiyin 千手千眼觀音像. Left-hand side: 修十王生七齋記, dated to 888 (transcribed p. 61) K29 (below K28, below the four characters 晚霞晨暉, on the right-hand side of the outer niche frame) 安祿山題龕, dated 713 (transcribed p. 62) K36: fragments of an inscription, illegible K45: fragments of inscriptions on the right- and left-hand side of the niche
南寒門K2: dated 1741
Visitor inscriptions (in 4 places); and K29 has the poem 刺安祿山 by the retired scholar Yuan Jushi 遠居士 of the Qing, followed by 安祿山題龕辯 by Zhu Shimin 朱時敏, dated 1229, who writes that the author of the former cannot be the famous An Lushan.
Chronology of the site: K1, K21, K22, K23 are the earliest, dated on style (mandorla) to Early Tang by comparison with Longmen, Middle Binyang Cave 賓陽中洞 (Bei Wei) . K43, K45; perhaps also Early Tang, comparable to Guangzuan Huangze Si cave 15, Guangyuan 劍閣櫎梁子 cave 2 and cave 6 . Other cave clusters are also dated on style. The Rice Granary Path 米倉道 connected Taipeng Shan and Bazhong 巴中
